stage.subtitle_ar: من فكرة بسيطة اسمها «البيانات» إلى جداولٍ منظّمة تسألينها فتُجيبكِ — هنا يبدأ قلب أي نظام برمجي.

content.intro_ar: قبل أن نتحدّث عن قواعد البيانات، لازم نفهم البيانات نفسها. البيانات هي كل شيء نخزّنه: اسمكِ، عمركِ، الصور، والرسائل. في تخصّص الحاسب ستعملين مع البيانات كل يوم، لذلك تعلّمي كلماتها بالإنجليزية من الآن. في هذا الدرس ستعرفين ما البيانات، وكيف يحوّلها الحاسوب إلى معلومات مفيدة.

vocab.meaning_en: facts, numbers, or text that a computer stores
vocab.example: The app saves your data on a server.

vocab.meaning_en: data that has meaning and is useful
vocab.example: The chart turns data into clear information.

vocab.meaning_en: a value you can count or measure
vocab.example: Her age is a number.

vocab.meaning_en: letters and words, like a name or a message
vocab.example: A name is text, not a number.

vocab.meaning_en: to keep data so you can use it later
vocab.example: Phones store photos and contacts.

vocab.meaning_en: one piece of data, like 18 or "Atyaf"
vocab.example: The value of her age is 18.

vocab.meaning_en: kept as numbers inside a computer
vocab.example: A digital photo is made of tiny dots.

vocab.meaning_en: to gather data from people or devices
vocab.example: The website collects your email.

vocab.meaning_en: to write down data, or one set of data
vocab.example: The app records the time of each visit.

vocab.meaning_en: one thing that shows what you mean
vocab.example: A phone number is an example of data.

paragraphs: Data is all around us. Your name, your age, and your photos are data. A phone number is data too. Every day, apps collect and store data about the things you do. Some data is text, like a name or a message. Some data is a number, like your age or the time.

But data alone is not always useful. When we give data meaning, it becomes information. For example, the number 18 is just a value. When we say "Atyaf is 18 years old," it becomes information. Computers are very good at this. They store digital data and turn it into clear information for people to read.

This is why data is so important in technology. Every website and every app records data. It stores your messages, your files, and your photos. As a future computer scientist, you will work with data every day, so it is good to know these words early. Data is the start of everything you will build.

key_phrases.en: This is an example of data.
A name is text, and an age is a number.

comprehension.question: What is data?
options: Facts, numbers, or text that a computer stores
comprehension.correct: Facts, numbers, or text that a computer stores
comprehension.explanation_ar: البيانات هي الحقائق والأرقام والنصوص التي يخزّنها الحاسوب.

comprehension.question: When does data become information?
comprehension.explanation_ar: تصبح البيانات معلومات عندما نمنحها معنى مفيدًا.

comprehension.question: Which one is text, not a number?
comprehension.explanation_ar: الاسم نص، بينما العمر والوقت والسنة أرقام.

comprehension.question: What do apps do every day?
comprehension.explanation_ar: التطبيقات تجمع البيانات وتخزّنها كل يوم.

comprehension.question: According to the reading, why is data important in technology?
options: Because every website and app records data
Because it is always a photo
comprehension.correct: Because every website and app records data
comprehension.explanation_ar: لأن كل موقع وكل تطبيق يسجّل البيانات، فهي أساس كل شيء.

task.prompt_en: Write 3–4 sentences about the data on your phone. What text and numbers does it store?
task.prompt_ar: اكتبي ٣–٤ جُمل عن البيانات الموجودة على جوّالكِ. ما النصوص والأرقام التي يخزّنها؟
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي ٣ كلمات على الأقل من كلمات الدرس مثل: data، store، number، text.

content.intro_ar: بعد أن عرفتِ ما البيانات، السؤال المهم هو: كيف نرتّبها؟ الجواب في معظم الأنظمة هو الجدول. الجدول يشبه الشبكة: صفوف وأعمدة، تمامًا كجدول في إكسل. في تخصّصكِ ستصمّمين جداول تخزّن آلاف السجلات بترتيب وأمان. في هذا الدرس ستتعلّمين أجزاء الجدول وكلماتها الإنجليزية التي يستخدمها كل مبرمج.

vocab.meaning_en: an organised place where a system stores its data
vocab.example: The school keeps student marks in a database.

vocab.meaning_en: data arranged in rows and columns
vocab.example: The students table holds one row per student.

vocab.meaning_en: a line across a table that holds one record
vocab.example: Each row is one student.

vocab.meaning_en: a line down a table for one kind of value
vocab.example: The email column holds every email.

vocab.meaning_en: all the data about one thing, stored in one row
vocab.example: Her record has her name, age, and email.

vocab.meaning_en: one value in a record, matching one column
vocab.example: The name field is empty for this record.

vocab.meaning_en: a unique value that names one record
vocab.example: The student ID is the primary key.

vocab.meaning_en: not the same as any other; appears only once
vocab.example: Every ID must be unique.

vocab.meaning_en: the kind of data a column holds, like text or number
vocab.example: The age column has a number type.

vocab.meaning_en: to plan how the tables and columns will look
vocab.example: Good design keeps data clean and clear.

vocab.meaning_en: to connect one table to another
vocab.example: We relate the orders table to the users table.

vocab.meaning_en: to arrange data in a clear, tidy way
vocab.example: Tables help you organise large amounts of data.

paragraphs: When a company has a lot of data, it needs a good way to organise it. The most common way is a database made of tables. A table looks like a grid, with rows and columns. If you have used Excel, you already know the idea. Each column has a name, such as "name," "age," or "email," and it holds one type of value.

Every row in a table is called a record. A record holds all the data about one thing — for example, one student. Each single value inside that record is a field. So Atyaf's record might have three fields: her name, her age, and her email. Because a table can store thousands of records, it must stay tidy and well designed.

There is one more important idea: the primary key. This is a special column that gives every record a unique value, so no two records are the same. A student ID is a good example, because each student has a different ID. Later, systems use these keys to relate one table to another. For instance, an "orders" table can point to a "users" table through the user's key. Learning to design clean tables is one of the first real skills of a computer scientist, and you will use it in almost every project you build.

key_phrases.en: The column holds one type of value.
key_phrases.ar: العمود يحمل نوعًا واحدًا من القيم.

key_phrases.en: The primary key must be unique.
key_phrases.ar: يجب أن يكون المفتاح الأساسي فريدًا.

key_phrases.en: We store the data in a table.
We relate one table to another.

comprehension.question: What is a table made of?
What do we call one row in a table?
What is a field?
comprehension.explanation_ar: الحقل هو قيمة واحدة داخل السِجِلّ، تقابل عمودًا واحدًا.

comprehension.question: Why must a primary key be unique?
options: So no two records are the same
comprehension.correct: So no two records are the same
comprehension.explanation_ar: المفتاح الأساسي فريد كي لا يتشابه سِجِلّان، فيُميِّز كل سِجِلّ بمفرده.

comprehension.question: What is a good example of a primary key?
comprehension.explanation_ar: رقم الطالب مثال جيد لأن لكل طالب رقمًا مختلفًا.

comprehension.question: How do systems connect two tables?
options: By using keys to relate them
comprehension.correct: By using keys to relate them
comprehension.explanation_ar: تستخدم الأنظمة المفاتيح لربط جدول بآخر.

task.prompt_en: Imagine a table for your favourite apps. Write 4–5 sentences: name three columns you would use, and describe one record in the table.
task.prompt_ar: تخيّلي جدولًا لتطبيقاتكِ المفضّلة. اكتبي ٤–٥ جُمل: سمّي ثلاثة أعمدة ستستخدمينها، وصِفي سِجِلًّا واحدًا في الجدول.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي على الأقل أربع كلمات من الدرس مثل: table، row، column، record، field، primary key.

content.intro_ar: الآن لديكِ جداول مليئة بالبيانات، فكيف تجدين ما تريدينه بينها؟ عبر الاستعلام (query): سؤال تكتبينه للحاسوب فيعيد لكِ الإجابة من قاعدة البيانات. لغة هذه الأسئلة اسمها SQL، وهي من أهم المهارات في تخصّصكِ. في هذا الدرس ستفهمين فكرة الاستعلام وكلماته الإنجليزية الأساسية، وستقرئين مثالًا بسيطًا بلغة SQL.

vocab.meaning_en: a question you ask a database to get data
vocab.example: This query returns all students in level B1.

vocab.meaning_en: the common language for asking databases questions
vocab.example: Most databases understand SQL.

vocab.meaning_en: to look through data to find something
vocab.example: You can search the table for one name.

vocab.meaning_en: the SQL word that chooses which columns to show
vocab.example: SELECT name FROM students.

vocab.meaning_en: to keep only the rows that match a rule
vocab.example: We filter the list to show only level B1.

vocab.meaning_en: the data that a query gives back to you
vocab.example: The result shows three rows.

vocab.meaning_en: a rule that decides which rows to include
vocab.example: The condition is age > 17.

vocab.meaning_en: to give data back as the answer
vocab.example: The query returns her email.

vocab.meaning_en: to put rows in order, such as A to Z
vocab.example: We sort the names from A to Z.

vocab.meaning_en: to be the same as what you asked for
vocab.example: Only two rows match the condition.

vocab.meaning_en: to change data that is already stored
vocab.example: We update her email to a new one.

paragraphs: A database can hold millions of records, so you need a fast way to find the right ones. You do this with a query. A query is simply a question that you ask the database, and the database sends back an answer called the result. Instead of reading every row yourself, you let the computer search for you.

Most databases use a language called SQL to write these questions. SQL reads a little like English. For example, the query "SELECT name FROM students WHERE level = 'B1'" means: show me the name from the students table, but only for the rows where the level is B1. The word SELECT chooses the column, and the word WHERE adds a condition. The database then returns only the rows that match.

Queries can do much more than find data. You can filter a table to keep only the rows you want, and you can sort the result so the names appear from A to Z. You can also update a record when something changes, such as a new email address. Because almost every app talks to a database, writing clear queries is a skill you will use for your whole career. Start with small, simple questions, and soon you will ask big ones with ease.

key_phrases.en: The database sends back a result.
We filter the rows by a condition.
Sort the names from A to Z.
key_phrases.ar: رتّبي الأسماء من الألف إلى الياء.

key_phrases.en: Only two rows match the condition.
We update the record with new data.

comprehension.question: What is a query?
options: A question you ask a database
comprehension.correct: A question you ask a database
comprehension.explanation_ar: الاستعلام هو سؤال تطرحينه على قاعدة البيانات لتحصلي على بيانات.

comprehension.question: What language do most databases use for queries?
comprehension.explanation_ar: معظم قواعد البيانات تستخدم لغة SQL لكتابة الاستعلامات.

comprehension.question: In the example, what does the word SELECT do?
options: It chooses which column to show
comprehension.correct: It chooses which column to show
comprehension.explanation_ar: كلمة SELECT تختار الأعمدة التي ستظهر في النتيجة.

comprehension.question: What does the WHERE part add to a query?
comprehension.explanation_ar: جزء WHERE يضيف شرطًا يحدّد الصفوف المطلوبة.

comprehension.question: What do we call the data a query gives back?
comprehension.explanation_ar: البيانات التي يُعيدها الاستعلام تُسمّى النتيجة (result).

comprehension.question: Which of these can a query also do?
comprehension.explanation_ar: الاستعلامات يمكنها أيضًا ترتيب البيانات وتحديثها.

task.prompt_en: In simple English, describe a question you would ask a database of your favourite songs (for example, "show all songs from 2024"). Write 3–4 sentences explaining what the query would search for and what result you expect.
task.prompt_ar: بالإنجليزية البسيطة، صِفي سؤالًا ستطرحينه على قاعدة بيانات لأغانيكِ المفضّلة (مثلًا: «أرِني كل الأغاني من عام ٢٠٢٤»). اكتبي ٣–٤ جُمل تشرحين ما الذي سيبحث عنه الاستعلام وما النتيجة المتوقّعة.
task.guidance_ar: استخدمي على الأقل ثلاث كلمات من الدرس مثل: query، search، filter، result، condition، sort.
